fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Texas Respiratory Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a respiratory therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Texas - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: respiratory therapist license reciprocity texas, how to transfer respiratory therapist license to texas, respiratory therapist compact texas, texas respiratory therapist endorsement application

board.name: Texas Medical Board — Respiratory Care
board.parent_dept: Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation (TDLR)
board.mailing_address: Texas Medical Board, P.O. Box 2018, Austin, TX 78768-2018

quick_facts.renewal_cycle: Every 2 years, based on birth month
quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) examination

license_types.title: Registered Respiratory Therapist (RRT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a respiratory therapist in Texas.

steps.title: Create Online Account
steps.description: Register for a new user account on the Texas Medical Board's Online Licensure System at login.tmb.state.tx.us. You will need to provide personal details and create login credentials.

steps.title: Complete Online Application
steps.description: Log in to the Online Licensure System, select the 'Respiratory Care Practitioner' application, and accurately complete all required sections of the application form.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the non-refundable application fee of $140 within the Texas Medical Board's Online Licensure System as part of your application submission.

steps.title: Submit Required Documentation
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts from your accredited respiratory care program and official NBRC score reports (for CRT/RRT examination) to be sent directly to the Texas Medical Board.

steps.title: Complete Criminal Background Check
steps.description: Follow the instructions provided within your application on the Online Licensure System to complete a fingerprint-based criminal background check through the Board's approved vendor, IdentoGO.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBRC. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Texas respiratory therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Texas have reciprocity for respiratory therapists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers respiratory therapist practice in Texas. To work in Texas, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my respiratory therapist license to Texas?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Texas while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Respiratory Therapist in Texas
hero.dek: To apply for a Respiratory Therapist license in Texas, submit your application through the Texas Medical Board's Online Licensure System.

official_links.portal_name: Texas Medical Board's Online Licensure System
official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the portal URL and click 'New User Registration' to create an account. You will need to provide personal information and set up security questions.
